description: Add hermetic vitest coverage for scripts/ralph.sh's deterministic surface (arg parsing, taskdesc validation, four-file contract) via black-box spawnSync tests, plus optional behavior-preserving source-guard unit tests for the resilience functions.

userStories.title: Black-box argument-validation tests
userStories.description: As a harness maintainer, I want ralph.sh's argument parsing covered by tests so that a regression in parse_args/normalize_harness fails CI instead of breaking the next autopilot run.
acceptanceCriteria: Create scripts/__tests__/ralph.test.ts modeled on scripts/__tests__/harness-config.test.ts (vitest, spawnSync, mkdtempSync/rmSync fixtures)

Add a run(args: string[], opts?: { env?: NodeJS.ProcessEnv, cwd?: string }) helper that invokes via spawnSync('bash', [SCRIPT, ...args], opts) — MUST be bash, not sh (ralph.sh uses [[ ]], BASH_SOURCE, arrays, PIPESTATUS) — returning { stdout, stderr, status }
Assert --harness=foo (unknown) exits 2 with stderr matching /unknown harness/
Assert --harness with no value AND no RALPH_HARNESS in the child env exits 2 with stderr matching /requires a value/
Assert zero positional args exits 2 (usage printed to stderr)
Assert more than one positional arg exits 2
Assert taskdesc not matching ^[a-z0-9-]+$ (e.g. Bad_Name) exits 2 with stderr matching /must match/
Tests pass (pnpm test:scripts); no pre-existing suite regresses
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Four-file contract tests
userStories.description: As a harness maintainer, I want the four-file contract enforcement tested so that a silent change to the required-files check is caught.
acceptanceCriteria: Hermeticity (load-bearing): spawn the child with cwd set to a non-git temp dir, e.g. spawnSync('bash', [SCRIPT, ...args], { cwd: tmpDir, env: { ...process.env, REPO_ROOT: tmpDir } }) — normal mode re-derives REPO_ROOT via git rev-parse at ralph.sh:358 and ignores the env var, so cwd (where git rev-parse fails and falls back to pwd) is what points the task-dir lookup at the fixture; mkdtempSync(os.tmpdir()) is non-git
A valid taskdesc with a missing tasks/<desc>/ dir exits 1 with stderr matching /does not exist/
With the task dir present but each of {prd.md, prd.json, prompt.md, progress.txt} omitted in turn, exits 1 with stderr matching /is missing/ (four separate assertions; a sub-loop is allowed)
No harness stub required: the task-dir check (ralph.sh:361) and four-file check (:369) run BEFORE harness resolution (:375); do NOT add a claude/codex stub for these cases
Tests are hermetic — no network, no claude/codex/pi/tmux dependency; the temp cwd keeps the real repo untouched
Tests pass (pnpm test:scripts)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Behavior-preserving source guard in ralph.sh (conditional)
userStories.description: As a harness maintainer, I want to source ralph.sh to call its functions in isolation, without launching the loop, so that the resilience functions can be unit-tested.
acceptanceCriteria: Modify scripts/ralph.sh so that source-ing it defines its functions WITHOUT executing the top-level normal-mode body (standard [ "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}" = "$0" ] idiom or equivalent)

The guard is a strict no-op for all three real invocation paths, each verified: (1) direct scripts/ralph.sh <slug> — bash scripts/ralph.sh Bad_Name still exits 2; (2) the internal bash "$SCRIPT_PATH" --loop re-entry (ralph.sh:279) still enters loop mode; (3) the no-tmux foreground fallback (ralph.sh:389) still transitions to --loop mode
If a clean guard proves infeasible (e.g. set -euo pipefail fires noisily on source), DROP this story, skip US-004 entirely, and leave ralph.sh unchanged (see Non-Goals conditional-drop branch)
Tests pass (pnpm test:scripts)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Completed iteration 3 on 2026-06-12; commit b57e746. Guard landed cleanly (no drop); all three invocation paths verified no-op, source defines functions without running body. US-004 GATE OPEN.

userStories.title: Resilience-function unit tests (depends on US-003)
userStories.description: As a harness maintainer, I want the throttle-detection and fallback logic unit-tested so that the autopilot's documented resilience guarantee is regression-guarded.
acceptanceCriteria: GATE: implement ONLY if US-003's source guard landed cleanly; if US-003 was dropped, skip US-004 entirely (no partial coverage)

claude_limit_detected ANDs two greps (ralph.sh:182: 'hit (your |the )?limit' AND 'resets?'): positive fixture contains BOTH phrases -> exit 0; a negative fixture with only ONE phrase -> exit 1 (proves AND-logic); ordinary output with neither -> exit 1

fallback_after_harness: with a fake codex on PATH, claude->codex and pi->codex; with codex absent, non-zero return + stderr matching /not on PATH/; an unsupported harness (e.g. opencode) -> non-zero return + stderr matching /no fallback/
normalize_harness: each of claude|pi|codex|opencode|deepagents echoes the value; an invalid value exits 2 with stderr matching /unknown harness/
PATH stubs use an executable shell file named codex (#!/usr/bin/env bash + exit 0) in a temp dir prepended to PATH
At least one positive and one negative assertion per function
Tests pass (pnpm test:scripts)
Typecheck passes
